x-enumeration-literals.name: Fee Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The cost charged by the factor for providing its services, typically a percentage of the invoice value.

A fee charged by the factoring company for its services. This fee is often expressed as a percentage of the total invoice value, usually 1-5%, and is deducted from the final payment after the customer settles the invoice.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Repayment Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
- In recourse factoring, the business must repay the factor if the customer fails to pay the invoice.

- In non-recourse factoring, the factoring company absorbs the loss if the customer does not pay, though this option comes with higher fees.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Collection Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
Who is responsible for collecting payments from customers.

The factor takes responsibility for collecting payments from the business's customers. This can involve notifying customers to pay the factor directly.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Assignment Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description: The agreement includes a process for the business to assign specific invoices to the factoring company. The factor reviews and approves which invoices will be factored.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Termination Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description: Conditions under which the factoring agreement can be terminated, including notice periods and any penalties for early termination.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Advance Rate Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The percentage of the invoice value that the factor advances to the business upfront.

This is typically between 70-90% of the invoice's value.

x-enumeration-literals.name: Account Reserve Arrangement
x-enumeration-literals.description:
The portion of the invoice value that is held back by the factor until the invoice is paid by the customer.

The remaining balance of the invoice value (usually 10-30%) held by the factor until the customer pays the invoice. Once the invoice is paid, the factor deducts its fee and releases the remaining funds to the business.
